title: Crisis contact documentation
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

red_flags.text: Supports documenting triage, current self-harm or suicide risk, leaving risk, capacity for a decision to leave, prior psychiatric history, mental-state examination and referral or follow-up when performed.
red_flags.rationale: Supports emergency mental-health documentation standards for mental-health crisis contact; no decision is generated.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports documenting triage, current self-harm or suicide risk, leaving risk, capacity for a decision to leave, prior psychiatric history, mental-state examination and referral or follow-up when performed.

source.title: Mental Health in Emergency Departments: A toolkit for improving care

exact_section.heading: Mental-health audit standards for the emergency department

red_flags.text: Supports recording that suicidal ideation, intent, support and immediate risk were directly assessed by a competent clinician; it does not populate a negative, assign risk, or generate escalation.
red_flags.rationale: Supports clinician-completed self-harm and suicide risk assessment for mental-health crisis contact; no decision is generated.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports recording that suicidal ideation, intent, support and immediate risk were directly assessed by a competent clinician; it does not populate a negative, assign risk, or generate escalation.

source.title: Depression in adults: treatment and management — Recommendations

treatment_and_management.text: Supports documenting current presenting state, agitation, immediate safety, safeguarding, willingness to accept care, observation level and care-plan status; it explicitly warns triage is not a future-risk prediction tool.
treatment_and_management.rationale: Supports mental-health triage and initial assessment fields for mental-health crisis contact; no decision is generated.
treatment_and_management.evidence_extract: Supports documenting current presenting state, agitation, immediate safety, safeguarding, willingness to accept care, observation level and care-plan status; it explicitly warns triage is not a future-risk prediction tool.

source.title: Mental Health in Emergency Departments: A toolkit for improving care

exact_section.heading: Mental Health Triage and Initial Assessment

source_limitations: All item-level mappings remain unsupported pending separate clinician review and signed approval.
Contact mode, location, presenting crisis, mental state, self-harm and violence risk, substances, supports, immediate actions, referrals and disposition remain absent.
No diagnosis, risk conclusion, investigation, treatment, medicine, dose, referral, escalation or disposition is generated for mental-health crisis contact.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
